title: WebNami vs Publii — A Web-Based Alternative to Publii Desktop CMS
description: Publii is a desktop-only static CMS. WebNami offers the same static-site benefits with a web-based admin you can access from any browser, no app install required.

hero.heading: Static site blogging without the desktop app
hero.subheading: Publii is a smart desktop CMS that generates static sites, but it ties you to one machine and requires an app install to manage your blog. WebNami delivers the same static-site speed through a browser-based admin you can use from any device.

reasons.heading: Access from any device
reasons.body: Publii requires its desktop app installed on one computer. WebNami's admin runs in a browser — manage your blog from your laptop, tablet, or another machine entirely.

reasons.heading: Faster to get started
reasons.body: Publii requires downloading and installing a desktop application. WebNami spins up with a single `npx` command — no installer, no setup wizard, just start writing.

reasons.heading: Markdown over SQLite
reasons.body: Publii stores content in a local SQLite database. WebNami stores everything as readable Markdown files, making content easy to version-control, diff, and back up.

reasons.heading: Modern CSS tooling
reasons.body: WebNami themes are built with Tailwind CSS v4 — the latest utility-first CSS framework. Customizing your design means editing real CSS, not a limited theme panel.
